Multiple Weather Station modules may be chained together to make more accurate predictions, allowing armies to prepare for even the worst of conditions.

WeaponRack.description: A rack used to mount multiple firearms.

WatchTower.description: Reports nearby enemy movements to players with Radios equipped

TankStop.description: An anti-tank obstacle that prevents vehicle access to an area. Can be dismantled with a Wrench.

StorageBox.description: A shared storage container that can be used to stash item.

ShippingContainer.description: A container for shipping very large quantities of Crates using Crane loaded vehicles. This type of container can only be unloaded at Storage Depots and Seaports.

Seaport.description: A large building used to store crates, vehicles, boats, and shippable structures.

ResourceBox.description: A mysterious box with a never ending supply of resources

ObservationTower.description: An Observation Tower that provides intel on friendly and enemy forces in an area.

MassProduction.description: A manufacturing plant for large-scale production of military equipment.

MaintenanceTunnel.description: Prevents the decay of nearby structures when supplied.

MagicBox.description: A magical box that duplicates the item in its first inventory slot.

MGPillbox.description: A well-fortified bunker position fitted with a mounted machinegun. Garrisoned infantry will lay down heavy suppressive fire upon approaching enemies from a fixed angle.

LRArtillery.description: A heavy fixed position artillery piece that can release devastating firepower on distant enemy targets.

Keep.description: An old Keep. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

IntelCenter.description: A high-tech listening post designed to intercept and decode enemy transmissions. The Intelligence Center can target distant locations and will continue to decipher sensitive transmissions over a sustained period.

ISGPillbox.description: This heavily reinforced pillbox allows the garrisoned infantry with a 30mm support gun to fire on approaching enemy armour from a safe position.

Hospital.description: A large medical facility for treating critically wounded soldiers returning from the battlefield.

GarrisonFishHut2.description: An abandoned building in the world that has been fortified and garrisoned.

FortLargeRadar.description: A radar that detects aircraft across a large effective radius and must be built atop a solid foundational hardpoint within bunker systems—for both operational stability and defence. The SC-3 Aerial Interceptor Array, or AIA, uses state-of-the-art technology to emit short radio bursts that provide aerial intelligence to nearby receivers and entrenched anti-aircraft defences.

FortEmpT3.description: A structural foundation for the building of Storm Cannons, Intelligence Centers, Weather Stations, and Underground Fortresses.

FirePitT2.description: This easy-to-construct range is perfect for keeping warm in the cold, while being sturdier than a simple firepit.

FirePitT1.description: A pit of fire used to keep soldiers warm during Snow Storms. Requires Diesel to fuel.

FieldPier.description: A temporary pier that facilitates movement of goods and troops onto large naval vessels from the shore.

FieldHospital.description: A field medical facility used to treat critically wounded soldiers at the front line.

FieldBridge.description: A makeshift bridge for transporting light equipment over a short distance. Will take damage when vehicles drive across.

Factory.description: A manufacturing plant for producing military equipment.

EngineeringCenter.description: A research facility where players can contribute towards the research of their faction's Tech Tree.

DeployedWindsockT.description: A sturdy fabric cylinder mounted on a tripod that’s designed to help nearby operators determine the speed and direction of wind.

DeployedListeningKit.description: A device used to intercept enemy radio broadcasts transmitted from nearby sources.

DeployedGrenadeLauncherTC.description: Capable of firing different grenades, quickly and at long range, the Fissura gd.I is mobile enough for easy transport, while increasing the effectiveness of infantry ordinance.

DeployedAirSirenT.description: A blaring siren with a distinctive tone that signifies a warning about impending airborne operations that's been deployed.

ConstructionYard.description: An area where certain structures can be built and prepared for shipping.

BarbedWireSpline.description: Used to slow down enemy infantry movement. Can be dismantled with a Wrench.

AircraftCrate.description: A crate for a packed aircraft frame.

ATPillbox.description: This reinforced pillbox fires a 14.5mm anti-tank rifle at approaching enemy infantry and vehicles.

GarrisonStation1.description: A garrisoned strongpoint used in the defence of a town. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

FortStructurePart.description: The second piece of a Large Structure. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

FortLargeRadarPart.description: The final critical components necessary to construct an SC-3 Aerial Interceptor Array. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

FortLRArtilleryPart.description: The final critical components necessary to construct a powerful Storm Cannon. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

FortIntelCenterPart.description: The final critical components necessary to construct a strategic Intelligence Center. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

FortGarrisonStationPart.description: The final critical components necessary to reinforce the basement of an Underground Fortress. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

ShipPart2.description: Highly resistant external plating used to protect naval vessels from damage and wear.

ShipPart1.description: Foundational elements required to construct the hulls of large naval vessels.

SandbagHalfSpline.description: A defensive wall that hinders enemy movement and provides cover during combat.

BarbedWireWallSpline.description: Used to prevent enemy infantry movement through an area. This structure is difficult to destroy with conventional weapons and must be dismantled with a Wrench.

RocketSite.description: A specialized launch platform designed to facilitate the assembly and firing of devastating A0E-9 Ballistic Missile, colloquially known as the AEON Rocket.

RocketPartTop.description: The A0E-9 Warhead is a devastating explosive, capable of taking out city districts or even entire small towns. It scorches the landscape, making it hard for the enemy to rebuild in the short-term.

RocketPartCenter.description: The body of an A0E-9 stores the fuel and instrumentation required to deliver the warhead over large distances.

RocketPartBottom.description: The control fins, fuel lines, and propulsion engines required for launch are all housed within the A0E-9 Booster system.

ObservationBunkerT3.description: A radio bunker that provides intel to friendly units about the surrounding area. Built from solid concrete, this reliable structure can push its effective range beyond lesser fortified radio structures, detects aircraft within range, and is equipped with binoculars that give occupants a greater field of view.

ObservationBunkerT2.description: A radio bunker that provides intel to friendly units about the surrounding area. It is capable of increased effectiveness, and is equipped with binoculars that give occupants a greater field of view.

FortFlameT2.description: A bunker with a mechanical station that will automatically spray water in the event of a fire. Includes a pipe connection.

FacilityVehicleFactory3.description: A floodable basin and construction platform that facilitates the assembly and repair of the largest naval vessels.

FacilityVehicleFactory2.description: A large Assembly Station for production of advanced vehicles and equipment, such as tanks and trains. Construction will take time and may be halted if resources are depleted.

FacilityVehicleFactory1.description: A standard Assembly Station for production of essential vehicles and equipment. Construction will take time and may be halted if resources are depleted. Some projects require a base vehicle to be present on the Assembly Station before production can begin.

FacilityTransferResource.description: An open container marked for storing raw resources that can be transferred into and out of Facilities. Stockpiles are public.

FacilityTransferMaterial.description: A space marked for storing refined materials that can be transferred into and out of Facilities. Stockpiles may be public or reserved.

FacilityTransferLiquid.description: Metal drums for storing various fuels that can be transferred into and out of Facilities. Stockpiles are public.

FacilityTransferCrate.description: This facility is for storing crates of equipment manufactured at nearby factories.

FacilitySiloOil.description: An industrial silo for storing various types of Fuel.

FacilityRoad.description: A temporary road used to enable vehicle access between main roads and remote bases.

FacilityRefineryOil.description: A refinery that processes Oil into other useful materials for the purposes of production and power generation.

FacilityRefineryCoal.description: A processing plant that refines Coal into useful materials used in facility production and power.

FacilityRefinery2.description: A factory for refining raw resources into Processed Construction Materials. Additional modifications can allow for additional refined materials needed for advanced facility operations.

FacilityRefinery1.description: A factory that refines raw resources into Construction Materials for advanced facilities. Additional modifications allow for the development of a variety of different materials.

FacilityPowerOil.description: A power plant that generates a large amount of electricity using Oil or Coal.

FacilityPowerDiesel.description: A structure that generates power by burning Diesel.

FacilityPipeValve.description: A weighted valve that controls the rate of flow through a pipeline. Requires a Wrench to adjust.

FacilityPipeUnderground.description: Industrial piping that allows for automatic transport of various liquids and fuels. These specialized pipes can be built underground to connect to pipe networks across areas with high traffic.

FacilityPipeOverhead.description: Industrial piping that allows for automatic transport of various liquids and fuels. These specialized pipes can be built overhead to connect to pipe networks across areas with high traffic.

FacilityPipe.description: Industrial piping that allows for automatic transport of various liquids and fuels into a network of pipes that connect to various facility structures.

FacilityModificationCenter.description: A garage that facilitates vehicle modifications and upgrades.

FacilityMineWater.description: Pumps water through pipes. Must be built on bodies of water.

FacilityMineResource4.description: A stationary harvester that automatically gathers Coal.

FacilityMineResource3.description: A stationary harvester that automatically gathers Sulfur.

FacilityMineResource2.description: A stationary harvester that automatically gathers Components.

FacilityMineResource1.description: A stationary harvester that automatically gathers Salvage.

FacilityMineOilRig.description: Offshore Platforms are open-water extraction facilities used to extract various oils. A steady supply of processed Coal is required for certain operations, as extracting materials from the ocean floor is a complex process.

FacilityMineOil.description: Extracts fuel from Oil Fields. Must be constructed nearby.

FacilityFactorySmallArms.description: A factory that specializes in manufacturing infantry uniforms. Modifications may be constructed to manufacture infantry arms and heavy ammunition.

FacilityFactoryAmmo.description: A factory for producing advanced ammo types such as large calibre shells, rockets, and flame ammo.

FacilityFactoryAircraft.description: A factory for producing and repairing advanced aircraft parts.

FacilityCrane.description: The Foreman is an all-purpose, no-frills, easy to assemble crane. Perfect for hauling heavy loads in facilities where loading and offloading is commonplace.

FacilityCatwalkStairs.description: Stairs that connect to raised Catwalks, which create paths above complex Facilities.

FacilityCatwalkPlatform.description: A raised walkway for creating paths above complex Facilities.

FacilityCatwalkBridge.description: A raised walkway for creating paths above complex Facilities.

ConstructionEquipment.description: An automated excavator that can be fuelled with Gravel to assist with construction projects.

ConcreteMixer.description: A portable device that mixes various materials to form Concrete, which are used to build heavily fortified structures.

WaterMineSpline.description: Designed for underwater deployment, Sea Mines detonate upon contact with enemy vessels.

TankMineSpline.description: Anti-tank minefield designed with a heavy payload for disabling enemy vehicles. Only visible when nearby.

SurfaceWaterMineSpline.description: A specialized sea minefield that floats near the surface, causing destructive damage to ship hulls.

MineSpline.description: A prepared area for creating anti-tank and anti-infantry minefields.

InfantryMineSpline.description: Anti-infantry minefield that deliver a fatal payload to unsuspecting soldiers. Only visible when nearby.

EmplacedMultiC.description: The DAE 3b-2 fires rockets over long distances from a safe, emplaced position. This unique weapon was given its name due to the screams heard across the battlefield during its first deployment, with some referring to the barrage as a “web of death.”

EmplacedLightArtilleryW.description: A light artillery cannon designed to be a fixture in defensive fortifications. The Lariat sports a formidable long-range 120mm cannon designed to put immense pressure on enemy infantry.

EmplacedInfantryW.description: The Snare Trap is a repurposed anti-aircraft flak cannon used to fortify emplaced positions with twin anti-infantry machine guns.

EmplacedInfantryC.description: Built like a saw blade, the DAE 1b-2 emplacement gun is fitted with three cascading machine gun turrets. What the "Serra" lacks in power, it makes up for in sheer rate of fire for a weapon of its size.

EmplacedIndirectC.description: To combat Caoivish ingenuity, the Meseans developed their own indirect RPG propulsion system. Dual launchers are fitted on a large emplacement platform to maximize coverage in an established position, rather than being relegated to a guerrilla warfare tool.

EmplacedHeavyArtilleryW.description: A heavy cannon designed to shatter the garrisons and fortifications of advancing forces. The Exalt is best utilized when emplaced into a defensive position to take advantage of its impressive range.

EmplacedHeavyArtilleryC.description: This heavy artillery cannon is designed to cripple enemy fortifications from an entrenched position. Its long heavy barrel gives the "Thunderbolt" outstanding range.

EmplacedCannonLargeC.description: Not to be outdone by its counterparts, the 2a-1 “Ruptura” is fitted with a 75mm cannon. Designed to aid in holding ground for longer durations, the “Ruptura” is a strong, efficient anti-armour tool.

EmplacedAircraftW.description: A late entry into the Leary arsenal, the AA-70 Bolas was a direct response to a sudden uptick in Colonial aerial aggression. This emplaced cannon excels equally at both slowing and bringing down enemy aircraft.

EmplacedAircraftC.description: A sturdy, stationary cannon for bombarding high-flying enemy aircraft with explosive shrapnel shells. The DAE 5b anti-aircraft emplacement has become infamous for walls of black smoke its payload leaves behind.

EmplacedATW.description: A defensive emplacement with a 68mm Anti-tank cannon. This rudimentary weapon was once built to fit onto the hull of large naval vessels, but was eventually phased out and repurposed.

EmplacedATLargeW.description: Built to compliment other free-standing artillery pieces, the emplaced Huber Starbreaker launches 94.5mm shells over very long distances. A necessary tool for infantry to knock back armoured Colonial advances.

Foxhole.description: A simple hole dug into the ground to provide infantry with makeshift cover from enemy fire.

TownBase3.description: A garrisoned focal building vital for the defence of a town. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

TownBase2.description: A garrisoned focal building vital for the defence of a town. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

TownBase1.description: A garrisoned focal building vital for the defence of a town. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

RelicBase1.description: An old fort. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

ForwardBase1.description: A temporary forward operating base. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

FortBaseT3.description: A large forward operating base. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

FortBaseT2.description: A large forward operating base. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

FortBaseT1.description: A large forward operating base. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

BorderBase.description: A small base used as a jump-off point for invading enemy regions. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

DeployedBannerTW.description: The Warden Ensign is a death mark for Caoiva’s enemies. Those who choose to ignore our warning will find that their bravery is misguided.

DeployedBannerTC.description: To plant a Vexillum of the Colonial Legion is to claim victory for the Mesean Republic. For the End is Our Glory.

BannerTW.description: The Warden Ensign is a death mark for Caoiva’s enemies. Those who choose to ignore our warning will find that their bravery is misguided.

BannerTC.description: To plant a Vexillum of the Colonial Legion is to claim victory for the Mesean Republic. For the End is Our Glory.

AircraftDepot.description: A large building used to store crates, vehicles, shippable structures, and aircraft.
